description:
If you're using Claude Code, Opencode, Codex CLI, or Gemini CLI in the terminal, you know how frustrating it is to downgrade to the mobile app when you're away from your computer. 

You lose your slash commands, sub-agents, MCP servers, skills, git access and more... everything that makes terminal agents powerful. 

In this video, I share my workflow to keep using your actual local terminal session from your phone, iPad, or any device using three simple, free tools: Tmux, Tailscale, and Termius.

I also show you the Claude hook I set up that sends me a Slack notification whenever Claude needs my attention—so I'm never waiting around wondering if it's done. 

Plus, I explain why I bought a Mac Mini and how it fits into this workflow (spoiler: it's not really for ClawdBot).

⏱️ TIMESTAMPS
0:00 – Introduction & the problem with mobile apps
1:00 – The three technologies: Tmux, Tailscale, Termius
2:00 – Demo: Switching from Mac to iPad mid-session
3:00 – Setting up Tmux
4:00 – Starting Tmux with Claude Code
5:00 – Setting up Tailscale & enabling SSH
6:00 – ClawdBot warning: use a separate machine
7:00 – Why I bought a Mac Mini (it's not for ClawdBot)
8:00 – Wrap up

🔗 RESOURCES
Tailscale: https://tailscale.com/
Tmux Wiki: https://github.com/tmux/tmux/wiki
Termius: https://termius.com/index.html
GSD (Get Shit Done) Framework: https://github.com/glittercowboy/get-shit-done

Book a call with me → https://yedatechs.com/#discovery-call
Sponsorship inquiries → hi@yedatechs.com

#ClaudeCode #CodexCLI #AgenticCLI #Tmux #Tailscale #DeveloperProductivity

The three technologies: Tmux, Tailscale, Termius
Demo: Switching from Mac to iPad mid-session
ClawdBot warning: use a separate machine
Why I bought a Mac Mini (it's not for ClawdBot)

fulltitle: Keep Using Your Local Claude Code Session from Any Device | Huge Unlock!
